content.problemStatement:
You are given three **distinct** integers A,B,A, B,A,B, and CCC.

Find **any** integer X (0≤X<230)X \ (0 \leq X < 2^{30})X (0≤X<230) such that:

(A⊕X)<(B⊕X)<(C⊕X)(A \oplus X) \lt (B \oplus X) \lt (C \oplus X)(A⊕X)<(B⊕X)<(C⊕X), where ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes the Bitwise XOR operation.

If no such XXX exists, print −1-1−1 instead.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT, the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains three space-separated integers A,B,A, B,A,B, and CCC.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single integer on a new line, the value of XXX that satisfies the above conditions or −1-1−1 if no such XXX exists.


If multiple such XXX satisfy the condition, you may print any.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: For X=4X = 4X=4,

A⊕X=1⊕4 =5A \oplus X = 1 \oplus 4 \ = 5A⊕X=1⊕4 =5
B⊕X=2⊕4 =6B \oplus X = 2 \oplus 4 \ = 6B⊕X=2⊕4 =6
C⊕X=3⊕4 =7C \oplus X = 3 \oplus 4 \ = 7C⊕X=3⊕4 =7

Thus, (A⊕X)<(B⊕X)<(C⊕X)(A \oplus X) \lt (B \oplus X) \lt (C \oplus X)(A⊕X)<(B⊕X)<(C⊕X).
Test case 222: No X (0≤X<230)X \ (0 \leq X \lt 2^{30})X (0≤X<230) exists that satisfies the above conditions.
Test case 333: For X=3X = 3X=3,

A⊕X=3⊕3 =0A \oplus X = 3 \oplus 3 \ = 0A⊕X=3⊕3 =0
B⊕X=2⊕3 =1B \oplus X = 2 \oplus 3 \ = 1B⊕X=2⊕3 =1
C⊕X=1⊕3 =2C \oplus X = 1 \oplus 3 \ = 2C⊕X=1⊕3 =2

Thus, (A⊕X)<(B⊕X)<(C⊕X)(A \oplus X) \lt (B \oplus X) \lt (C \oplus X)(A⊕X)<(B⊕X)<(C⊕X).
